summary: Backend testing for ScoreBlast complete. All 24 pytest tests PASSED (100%). Verified Excel upload (insert/re-upload skip/day rollover/missing required skip/estimated odds), matches CRUD with day & q filters, Claude Sonnet 4.5 prediction (returns JSON with family/main_prediction/confidence/analysis/playable_markets, cached on 2nd call), single + bulk results, selection toggle/clear, export/import (incremental dedup on sq1+sq2+day, no MongoDB _id leaks), AI Studio CSV prompt, and delete-all. No ObjectId serialization errors detected.
critical_code_review_comments: REQUIRED_ODDS includes odd_O25 so estimate_missing's Poisson derivation of O1.5/O3.5 from O2.5 is always applicable - good design.
Route ordering correct: /matches/days, /matches/selected/list, /matches/all are defined BEFORE /matches/{match_id} so no path-param shadowing occurs (verified by passing tests).
No input validation on result field (e.g. '2-1') - accepts any string. Low priority but a regex like ^\d+-\d+$ would harden the API.
EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is read at import time via os.environ.get with empty default; if key were missing /predict would return 500 - acceptable behavior.
Backend file is 648 lines - within reasonable limit but approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting parser/AI/routes into separate modules if more endpoints are added.
All endpoints correctly use projection {'_id': 0} to exclude MongoDB ObjectId - no serialization errors observed.
seed_data_creation: All seed data created in-test (TEST_ prefixed teams). conftest.py module-level fixture deletes all matches before and after the test module to ensure clean state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully validated. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_scoreblast_api.py is reusable for regression. Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Excel fixtures generated in-memory via openpyxl. Prediction tests hit live Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Emergent LLM (~5s for first call, cached after). For frontend testing, mobile viewport 375x667 recommended and the app is an Expo web preview.
